title: T-117 — Structured logger (pino) with secret redaction re-exported from @monopilot/observability
prompt:
# T-117 — Structured logger (pino) with secret redaction

PRD: docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md §5 Cross-cutting infra (Observability), §11 Audit log (security/standard/operational/ephemeral retention), §13 Niefunkcjonalne
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Add a pino-based structured logger to `@monopilot/observability` (T-116) that redacts a curated allowlist of secret-bearing keys before any line is written. Today every module assumes `logger.*` exists; auditor B Top-10 Gap #3 and §4.10 confirm no logger/metrics/tracing package exists. This task delivers the JSON logger half of the observability triple; OTel was T-116; Sentry is T-118.

## Implementation contract
1. Extend `packages/observability` with `src/logger.ts` exporting `createLogger(opts?: { name?: string; level?: string; redactKeys?: string[] }): Logger` returning a pino instance configured with:
   - default level `process.env.LOG_LEVEL ?? 'info'`,
   - default redact paths covering: `password`, `pin`, `token`, `access_token`, `refresh_token`, `mfa_secret`, `scim_token_hash`, `recovery_codes`, `private_jsonb`, `authorization`, `cookie`, `set-cookie`, `database_url` (passwords inside URLs — pino's `*.password` glob), `subject_id` (GDPR PII guard from T-114), `actor_user_id` (mask to first 8 chars).
   - `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test'` → pretty pino-pretty transport.
2. Re-export from `packages/observability/src/index.ts`: `export { createLogger, type Logger } from './logger'`.
3. Replace the shim logger in `apps/worker/src/logger.ts` (T-111) with `import { createLogger } from '@monopilot/observability'; export const logger = createLogger({ name: 'worker' });`. The shim's exported shape is preserved.
4. Add `packages/observability/src/__tests__/logger.test.ts`:
   - Capture pino output to an in-memory destination (`pino.destination` with `pino.streams.send`-like custom write).
   - Log `logger.info({ password: 'p4ss', token: 'tok', database_url: 'postgres://u:secret@h/db', user: { actor_user_id: 'abc-123-very-long' }, payload: 'ok' }, 'msg')`.
   - Assert the emitted line contains `"password":"[Redacted]"`, `"token":"[Redacted]"`, the database_url password section masked, the actor_user_id either masked or replaced with the short prefix, and `"payload":"ok"` and `"msg":"msg"` intact.
   - Assert level filtering works (level=warn drops .info).
5. Document the redact-keys allowlist in `packages/observability/README.md` (create) and add a note in `_foundation/contracts/gdpr.md` (T-113) that the logger MUST be used for any code path touching `subject_id` (avoid `console.log` of erasure rows).

## Files in scope
- packages/observability/src/logger.ts [create]
- packages/observability/src/index.ts [modify]
- packages/observability/src/__tests__/logger.test.ts [create]
- packages/observability/README.md [create]
- packages/observability/package.json [modify — add pino, pino-pretty deps]
- apps/worker/src/logger.ts [modify — replace shim with re-export]
- _foundation/contracts/gdpr.md [modify — append redaction note]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given logger created via createLogger({name:'test'}), when logger.info({password:'p4ss', token:'tok', payload:'ok'}, 'msg') called, then captured output contains "password":"[Redacted]", "token":"[Redacted]", "payload":"ok", "msg":"msg".
2. Given logger with redactKeys containing database_url, when value matching postgres://user:secret@host/db logged, then password section masked while rest of URL visible.
3. Given actor_user_id in redact allowlist, when logged, then emitted value is either [Redacted] or first-8-char prefix (document the choice).
4. Given LOG_LEVEL=warn, when logger.info('skip') called, then nothing written to destination; AND Given apps/worker/src/logger.ts re-imported by JobRegistry, when worker logs, then output is JSON lines with name='worker' and redact allowlist applied.
## Test strategy
- RED: write `logger.test.ts` with 5 scenarios before implementation. Capture pino output via a custom Writable. RED command: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/observability test`.
- GREEN: implement logger + redact paths + re-export + worker shim swap.
- Run: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/observability test && pnpm --filter @monopilot/worker test`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not use `console.log` anywhere in production code paths once this lands — replace with the logger; ESLint rule additions to `tooling/eslint/base.mjs` are P1 follow-up (out-of-scope this task, but called out in CLOSEOUT).
- Do not redact the entire payload — redact only the allowlisted keys; over-redaction destroys debuggability.
- Do not log the entire `req.headers` — `authorization` and `cookie` MUST be redacted; the redact list covers this.
- Do not log the `subject_id` of a GDPR erasure request — the redact list covers it; the contract note in `_foundation/contracts/gdpr.md` is normative.
- Do not synchronous-flush in production hot paths — pino is async by default, do not change.
- Do not introduce a second logger package — single source-of-truth in `@monopilot/observability`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Add pino logger to @monopilot/observability with curated redact allowlist (password/pin/token/subject_id/actor_user_id/database_url credentials/etc). Replace apps/worker shim. Vitest asserts redaction round-trip + level filtering.
pipeline_inputs.details:
pino default level via LOG_LEVEL env. Redact paths: password, pin, token, access_token, refresh_token, mfa_secret, scim_token_hash, recovery_codes, private_jsonb, authorization, cookie, set-cookie, database_url password section, subject_id, actor_user_id (masked to 8-char prefix). NODE_ENV=test uses pino-pretty. createLogger accepts redactKeys to extend allowlist per-package. Test captures via custom Writable destination.

AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F7 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost).
acceptance_criteria: Given logger created via createLogger({name:'test'}), when logger.info({password:'p4ss', token:'tok', payload:'ok'}, 'msg') called, then captured output contains "password":"[Redacted]", "token":"[Redacted]", "payload":"ok", "msg":"msg".
Given logger with redactKeys containing database_url, when value matching postgres://user:secret@host/db logged, then password section masked while rest of URL visible.
Given actor_user_id in redact allowlist, when logged, then emitted value is either [Redacted] or first-8-char prefix (document the choice).

Given LOG_LEVEL=warn, when logger.info('skip') called, then nothing written to destination; AND Given apps/worker/src/logger.ts re-imported by JobRegistry, when worker logs, then output is JSON lines with name='worker' and redact allowlist applied.
test_strategy: RED: write logger.test.ts with 5 scenarios before implementation; capture pino output via custom Writable; RED command: pnpm --filter @monopilot/observability test.
GREEN: implement logger + redact paths + re-export + worker shim swap.
risk_red_lines: Do not use console.log anywhere in production code paths once this lands — ESLint rule is P1 follow-up.
Do not redact the entire payload — only allowlisted keys.
Do not log req.headers without redact — authorization and cookie covered by allowlist.
Do not log subject_id of GDPR erasure request — covered by allowlist; contract note is normative.
Do not synchronous-flush in production hot paths — keep pino async default.
Do not introduce a second logger package — single source-of-truth in @monopilot/observability.
